title: Objective Summary
description: Assessment of the concept that an objective summary restates central idea and key points without opinion.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes an objective summary?

choices.text: A short restatement of the central idea and key points without personal opinion
choices.feedback: This is correct because an objective summary focuses on the text's main ideas and avoids judgment.

choices.text: A list of every detail the reader can remember
choices.feedback: A summary should be selective, not a full list of details.

choices.text: A paragraph that explains whether the reader liked the text
choices.feedback: Personal opinion does not belong in an objective summary.

choices.text: A sentence copied directly from the introduction
choices.feedback: Copying one sentence does not necessarily summarize the whole text objectively.

questions.prompt: A student writes this summary of an article about community gardens: "The article explains that community gardens provide fresh food, build neighborhood cooperation, and improve unused spaces." Why is this a strong objective summary?

choices.text: It includes the main idea and key points without adding opinion
choices.feedback: This is correct because the summary states the article's meaning clearly and remains neutral.

choices.text: It uses a dramatic tone to persuade the reader
choices.feedback: An objective summary should not try to persuade with dramatic language.

choices.text: It focuses only on the least important detail
choices.feedback: The summary actually addresses broad key points, not one minor detail.

choices.text: It explains how the student personally feels about gardens
choices.feedback: The statement does not include personal feelings, which is why it stays objective.

questions.prompt: Which sentence makes a summary less objective?

choices.text: The article states that later start times improve student focus.
choices.feedback: This remains objective because it reports the article's idea without judgment.

choices.text: The author explains that students get more sleep when school begins later.
choices.feedback: This also stays objective because it restates content from the text.

choices.text: Clearly, every school should adopt this brilliant plan immediately.
choices.feedback: This is correct because words like clearly and brilliant add opinion instead of objective summary.

choices.text: The article connects school schedules to attention in class.
choices.feedback: This is a neutral restatement of the article's message.

questions.prompt: After reading a text about recycling programs, which summary is most objective?

choices.text: The article explains how recycling programs reduce waste, lower disposal costs, and encourage responsible habits.
choices.feedback: This is correct because it captures the central idea and key points without opinion.

choices.text: The article is exciting because recycling is obviously the smartest policy.
choices.feedback: This includes judgment and persuasive language, so it is not objective.

choices.text: Recycling bins are blue, and one school bought thirty of them last year.
choices.feedback: This is too narrow because it focuses on isolated details rather than the main idea.

choices.text: I think the article should have included more interviews with students.
choices.feedback: This is the reader's opinion, not a summary of the text.

questions.prompt: True or False: An objective summary should avoid personal reactions and focus on the text's central idea and key points.

choices.feedback: This is correct because objective summaries restate what the text says without personal opinion.
This is incorrect because adding personal reactions makes a summary less objective.
